title: Pastéis de Nata with Red Bean Filling
description: A traditional Portuguese dessert with a twist, featuring a red bean filling inside an indulgent pastry.

ingredients.name: Pastry
ingredients.notes: Store-bought or homemade pastry for the crust

ingredients.name: Red Bean Paste
ingredients.notes: Available at most Asian markets or online

ingredients.name: Granulated Sugar
ingredients.notes: To balance the savory flavor of the red bean paste

ingredients.name: Egg
ingredients.notes: For egg wash to glaze the pastry

instructions: Roll out the pastry to a thickness of 1/8 inch (3 mm).
Cut out circles of pastry using a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass.
Place a tablespoon of red bean paste in the center of each pastry circle.
Fold the pastry in half to form a triangle and press the edges to seal.
Use a fork to crimp the edges of the pastry.
Brush the tops of the pastries with egg wash for a golden glaze.
Place the pastries on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Dot the top of each pastry with a small amount of vegan butter.
Bake the pastries for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown.
Allow the pastries to cool for 5 minutes before serving.
Optional: Top with aerated whipped cream and serve immediately.
